courses.title: Python Programming for young advanced hackers
courses.description: A hands-on, project-based introduction to coding for beginners, kids, and teens, covering basics to object-oriented programming.

chapters.title: Getting Ready for Python Programming & Hello World
chapters.description: Introduction to programming, running Python in the browser, and writing your very first console programs with print() and comments.

chapters.title: Variables, Numbers, and Simple Math
chapters.description: Store numbers in variables, use arithmetic operators, and build basic math expressions that print results to the console.

chapters.title: Function Basics and Built-ins
chapters.description: See functions as helpers, call built-in functions like print(), len(), and round(), and read their results from the console.

chapters.title: Data Types and Type Conversion
chapters.description: Work with integers, floats, strings, and booleans, convert between them, and understand how types affect operators and printed output.

chapters.title: Execution Flow and Debugging
chapters.description: Follow how Python runs code line by line, trace variables, and use print-debugging to understand and fix program behavior.

chapters.title: Operators and Comparisons
chapters.description: Use arithmetic, comparison, assignment, and logical operators to build expressions and decision conditions that drive printed results.

chapters.title: Numbers, Math Library, and Randomness
chapters.description: Do more advanced math with the math module, exponents, rounding, and controlled randomness for simple console-based simulations.

chapters.title: Working with Text: Strings
chapters.description: Create and manipulate strings, use indexing and slicing, apply string methods, and format output using f-strings.

chapters.title: Lists, Tuples, and Dictionaries
chapters.description: Store collections of data using lists, tuples, and dictionaries, and access, update, and iterate over their items with printed feedback.

chapters.title: Sets and Basic Data Operations
chapters.description: Use sets to handle unique items, perform union and intersection, and combine sets with other collections in console-based programs.

chapters.title: Conditional Logic: If, Elif, Else
chapters.description: Make decisions in your programs using if/elif/else, nested conditions, and logical operators to control what gets printed.

chapters.title: Loops and Iteration Patterns
chapters.description: Repeat work using for and while loops, range(), and common patterns for iterating over strings and collections, with visible console output.

chapters.title: Functions, Parameters, and Return Values
chapters.description: Define your own functions with parameters and return values, use default arguments, and verify behavior by printing function calls.

chapters.title: Comprehensions, map, filter, and zip
chapters.description: Use list/dict/set comprehensions and functional tools like map(), filter(), and zip() to transform and combine collections and print the results.

chapters.title: Object-Oriented Python: Classes and Objects
chapters.description: Learn the basics of object-oriented Python: defining classes, creating objects, and using attributes and methods with console-based examples.

courses.title: SQL Data Hacking for Young Pros
courses.description: Master the language of data. A hands-on, project-based course using SQLite to query, analyze, and build databases from scratch.

chapters.title: The Data Matrix: Intro to SQLite & Tables
chapters.description: Understand what a database is, how SQLite works, and run your first query to peek inside a table.

chapters.title: Selecting Your Target: The SELECT Statement
chapters.description: The 'Hello World' of SQL. Learn to retrieve specific columns, use aliases, and fetch all data with the wild card (*).

chapters.title: Filtering the Noise: The WHERE Clause
chapters.description: Filter results to find exactly what you need using equality, inequality, and comparison operators.

chapters.title: Advanced Search Patterns: LIKE and Logical Operators
chapters.description: Combine conditions with AND/OR/NOT and use wildcard pattern matching (LIKE) to find text that 'looks like' your query.

chapters.title: Ranking and Slicing: ORDER BY and LIMIT
chapters.description: Sort your data alphabetically or numerically and limit the output to see only the top-tier results.

chapters.title: Computed Columns and Nulls
chapters.description: Perform basic math directly in your queries, handle missing data (NULL), and format your output results.

chapters.title: Data Intelligence: Aggregation Functions
chapters.description: Don't just read data, understand it. Use COUNT, MAX, MIN, SUM, and AVG to get statistics from your tables.

chapters.title: Categorizing Data: GROUP BY and HAVING
chapters.description: Group rows into buckets to summarize data by category and filter those groups using the HAVING clause.

chapters.title: Inserting New Data: The INSERT Statement
chapters.description: Stop just looking and start creating. Learn how to add single or multiple rows of new data into your tables.

chapters.title: Modifying History: UPDATE and DELETE
chapters.description: Learn the dangerous art of modifying existing records and permanently deleting data from your database.

chapters.title: Architecting the Base: CREATE TABLE and Data Types
chapters.description: Design your own schema. Create tables, define column data types (INTEGER, TEXT, REAL), and understand constraints.

chapters.title: The Relational Model: Keys and Constraints
chapters.description: Understand how tables talk to each other using Primary Keys and Foreign Keys to keep data integrity.

chapters.title: Connecting Tables: INNER and LEFT JOINS
chapters.description: The power of SQL lies in connections. Merge data from multiple tables into a single result set using JOINs.

chapters.title: Inception Queries: Subqueries
chapters.description: Write queries inside queries. Use the result of one select statement to drive the filter or logic of another.

chapters.title: Optimization and Views
chapters.description: Save complex queries as virtual tables (Views) and learn the basics of Indexes to make your searches lightning fast.

courses.title: HTML Basics for Young Pros
courses.description: A beginner-friendly course introducing HTML fundamentals, structure, and best practices.

chapters.title: What is HTML?
chapters.description: What HTML means and what it does or doesn't do; contrast with CSS and JavaScript.

chapters.title: Requirements
chapters.description: Tools you need: code editor, browser, and creating your first HTML file.

chapters.title: HTML Document Structure
chapters.description: DOCTYPE, html, head, and body elements and their roles.

chapters.title: Head & Meta
chapters.description: Using title, meta charset, and viewport meta tags correctly.

chapters.title: Headings
chapters.description: Heading elements h1 to h6 and their use for structure and SEO.

chapters.title: Paragraphs & Text
chapters.description: Paragraphs, line breaks, horizontal rules, and text emphasis tags.

chapters.title: Links
chapters.description: Anchor tags, internal vs external links, and using target attributes.

chapters.title: Images
chapters.description: The img element, src and alt attributes, and image path basics.

chapters.title: Lists
chapters.description: Unordered and ordered lists with list items.

chapters.title: Tables
chapters.description: Basic table structure with table, tr, td, and th elements.

chapters.title: Divs & Spans
chapters.description: Difference between block and inline elements using div and span.

chapters.title: Semantic HTML
chapters.description: Using semantic elements like header, nav, main, section, article, and footer.

chapters.title: Form Basics
chapters.description: The form element and simple inputs with labels.

chapters.title: Form Inputs Overview
chapters.description: All form input types: text, email, password, checkbox, radio, textarea, select.

chapters.title: Attributes
chapters.description: id, class, title, placeholder, required, disabled and how to use them.

chapters.title: HTML Best Practices
chapters.description: Indentation, readable code, and HTML comments.

chapters.title: Accessibility Basics
chapters.description: Alt text, form labels, and semantic markup for accessibility.

chapters.title: SEO Basics
chapters.description: Using headings, meta description, and proper structure for search engines.

chapters.title: HTML + CSS
chapters.description: Linking stylesheets and what CSS is used for.

chapters.title: Why JavaScript and CSS Matter
chapters.description: How to connect JS and CSS, and a recap of frontend vs backend.

courses.title: CSS Basics for Young Pros
courses.description: A beginner-friendly CSS course for kids and teens, focused on styling web pages with clear examples.

chapters.title: What Is CSS and Why Use It?
chapters.description: How CSS improves HTML pages and the difference between styling and structure.

chapters.title: Adding CSS to Your HTML
chapters.description: Inline styles, internal styles, and external style sheets.

chapters.title: Understanding Selectors and Properties
chapters.description: How to target elements and apply basic styling rules.

chapters.title: Colors and Units
chapters.description: Color values (name, HEX, RGB, HSL) and sizes (px, %, em, rem).

chapters.title: Basic Text Styling
chapters.description: Font families, sizes, weights, and colors.

chapters.title: Text Alignment and Spacing
chapters.description: Controlling line height, alignment, and letter spacing.

chapters.title: Styling Links
chapters.description: Changing link colors, hover effects, and removing underlines.

chapters.title: The CSS Box Model Explained
chapters.description: Content, padding, border, and margin.

chapters.title: Box Sizing and Borders
chapters.description: How borders and box-sizing affect layout.

chapters.title: Adding Backgrounds
chapters.description: Colors, gradients, and images as backgrounds.

chapters.title: Display and Positioning
chapters.description: Block vs. inline elements, and positioning (static, relative, absolute, fixed).

chapters.title: Flexbox for Beginners
chapters.description: Aligning and spacing elements easily with flexbox.

chapters.title: Introduction to CSS Grid
chapters.description: Creating simple grids for modern layouts.

chapters.title: Shadows and Borders
chapters.description: Drop shadows for depth and rounded corners for smooth design.

chapters.title: Transitions and Simple Animations
chapters.description: Making hover and color effects come alive.

chapters.title: What Is Responsive Design?
chapters.description: Making your page look good on phones, tablets, and desktops.

chapters.title: Using Media Queries
chapters.description: Changing layout and font sizes for different screen widths.

chapters.title: Organizing Your CSS
chapters.description: Keeping code readable, using comments and indentation.

chapters.title: Reusing Styles with CSS Variables
chapters.description: Making color themes and reusable parameters.

chapters.title: Quick Intro to Frameworks
chapters.description: What frameworks are (like Bootstrap or Tailwind) and when to try them.

chapters.title: CSS Reference: Selectors, Properties, and Values
chapters.description: A structured reference chapter listing CSS building blocks in one place.

courses.title: Git, GitHub, and VS Code Basics for Young Students
courses.description: A beginner-friendly course that teaches the basics of Git version control, GitHub collaboration, and how to use VS Code with Git.

chapters.title: Version Control, Git, and GitHub
chapters.description: What version control is, what Git does locally, and what GitHub adds online.

chapters.title: Setting Up Git and VS Code
chapters.description: Configure your Git identity and learn the key Git features inside VS Code.

chapters.title: Your First Repo: Status, Add, Commit
chapters.description: See changes, stage the right files, write clear commits, and learn what `.gitignore` is for.

chapters.title: Branches, Merging, and Conflicts
chapters.description: Work in feature branches, merge safely, and understand how to resolve merge conflicts in VS Code.

chapters.title: GitHub Remotes: Clone, Push, Pull
chapters.description: Sync your work with GitHub using clone, push, pull, and the `origin` remote.

chapters.title: Collaboration: Pull Requests, Reviews, and Issues
chapters.description: Use pull requests, reviews, and issues to collaborate without overwriting each other.

chapters.title: Undoing Mistakes (Without Panic)
chapters.description: Learn safe ways to undo changes with restore, revert, stash, and reflog basics.

chapters.title: Good Habits: Clean History and Clear Messages
chapters.description: Write good commit messages, keep commits focused, and learn simple tagging and documentation habits.
